landing.standfirst: A deterministic daily read on where the cycle stands — fund flows across sectors and a Weinstein-stage positioning call, delivered every morning before the open. Open research, free to read.
landing.sampleHeadline: Tech accumulates while energy bleeds — cycle holds at Phase 3
landing.sampleDeck: Rotation is concentrating into a single leadership lane. That narrowing is the tell, not the breakout.
landing.sampleBody: The composite read is unchanged week over week: a mid-to-late expansion that rewards trend but punishes chasing. Sector dispersion is widening — a handful of names carry the tape while the breadth underneath thins. We treat this as a confirmer of the regime already in place, not a prediction of the next one.
landing.glanceNote: Open research, free. The full fund-flow table and cycle breakdown are public — for everyone.
landing.ctaBody: The full daily dispatch, the weekly fund-flow table, and the complete archive are open and free. Contributions welcome.

pricing.deliveryNote: Dispatches target 00:00 UTC. Occasional delays happen — a late day is a known property, not a broken promise.

paper.gateBody: How SightLab reads the cycle — fund-flow accumulation/distribution, Weinstein staging with the 30-week SMA and sector dispersion, and the confirmer-not-predictor framing. Free to read with an account.

dispatch.staleBanner: Today's edition hasn't arrived yet — showing the {date} dispatch.

s6.proxyFootnote: All A/D reads in this table are volume-price inference. Crypto ETFs also publish real creation/redemption flows — when the two disagree, trust the real flows.
s6.weakFootnote: (weak) = a marginal signal just past the threshold — context only; conclusions rest on strong signals.

cx.tag: CYCLE — LEADING & METHOD · read alongside the cycle read above
cx.sleeveFrame: Lower-confidence, faster, noisier than the cycle read — an early-warning lean, not a forecast. The composite above stays the anchor.
cx.blockvoteFrame: Block-vote de-weights correlated layers. When its implied phase diverges from the headline above, part of the headline is correlated-cluster double-counting — shown in the open, not hidden.
cx.dwell: in this phase for {n, plural, one {# reading} other {# readings}}

glossary.title: How to read this dispatch
glossary.intro: This dispatch is built from Weinstein stages, the 30-week moving average, and sector dispersion. Here is what the table terms mean, so a first-time reader can decode them.

ladders.def: This page uses two different four-step ladders. The page-top cycle read uses Templeton's sentiment phases — bull markets are born in pessimism, grow on skepticism, mature on optimism, die on euphoria — labelled Phase. The sector table uses Weinstein's trend Stages: basing, advance, top, decline. The numbers overlap but the meanings differ — Phase 3 is optimism, while Stage 3 is a topping pattern — so the two words are kept distinct on purpose.

stage.def: Weinstein splits a trend into four Stages: Stage 1 basing (flat average), Stage 2 advance (rising average, price above it), Stage 3 top (the average flattens, momentum fades), and Stage 4 decline (falling average, price below it). It answers: which part of the larger arc are we in?

distance.def: How far price sits above or below its 30-week simple moving average (roughly seven months of closes), in %. +20% means price is 20% above the average — the more extended, the further from trend; near 0 means it is hugging the line, the weakest trend.

slope.def: How fast the 30-week average line itself is rising or falling. Distance measures how far price is from the line; slope measures how steeply the line tilts. A steeper slope is a stronger trend; a flattening slope is the classic Stage-2-into-Stage-3 (topping) tell.

dispersion.def: How spread out the sectors are across stages and trend strength. Low means broad agreement — healthy breadth and a more trustworthy signal; high is a yellow flag — leadership is narrowing to a few names, the classic late-expansion fragility.

flows.def: From classical volume-price analysis. Accumulation is a sector being bought persistently (net inflow); distribution is being sold persistently (net outflow). It describes who controls the tape — not a forecast of tomorrow — and it lags by design.

volume.def: This week's average daily volume versus the previous week's, in %. −30% means the week traded on 30% thinner volume day to day. It measures participation, not price: an advance on shrinking volume is weaker evidence than the same advance on expanding volume.

confidence.def: How consistently the evidence layers behind the cycle read point the same way. High means the layers agree; Low means they conflict. It measures the internal coherence of today's read — not the probability that the market keeps moving in that direction.
